popularStory.headline: History of Ermengarde de Beaumont, Queen of Scotland (1170-1233)
popularStory.description: Ermengarde de Beaumont (c. 1170 – 11 February 1233) was Queen of Scotland as the wife of King William I. She is reported to have exerted influence over the affairs of state as queen, though the inform …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel d'Avenel於1143年出生在蘇格蘭艾丁波時，她的父親Robert Avenel27歲，母親Sybil23歲。她和William 'The Lion' King of Scotland至少有7個女兒。她去世於1235年在蘇格蘭斯林史爾斯特林享年92歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭費伏巴爾莫利諾。

nameMeanings.name: d'Avenel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from Ravenel, the name of several places in various parts of France, e.g. in Oise, derived from Old French ravene (from Latin raphanus) ‘horseradish’.
History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Ravenelle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gaetan, Normand, Raoul.
French: variant of   Ravenel   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
History: Christophe Ravenelle dit Lalime, a French soldier from Nancy, Lorraine, who married Agathe Charron in QC in 1758, was born Ravenel. Some of his descendants bear the surname Lalime .

variants.name: Ravenell
meanings: Possibly also a rare variant of English Ravenhill or its cognates Ravenhall or Revnell.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella

It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel d'Avenel was born in 1143, in Edinburgh, Scotland, her father, Robert Avenel, was 27 and her mother, Sybil, was 23. She had at least 7 daughters with William 'The Lion' King of Scotland. She died in 1235, in Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, at the age of 92, and was buried in Balmerino, Fife, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel d'Avenel est née en 1143, à Edinburgh, Écosse, son père, Robert Avenel, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Sybil, avait 23 ans. Elle a eu au moins 7 filles avec William 'The Lion' King of Scotland. Elle est décédée en 1235, à Stirling, Stirlingshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 92 ans, et a été enterrée à Balmerino, Fife, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel d'Avenel 1143 in Edinburgh, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert Avenel 27 und ihre Mutter Sybil 23. Sie hatte mit William 'The Lion' King of Scotland mindestens 7 Töchter. Sie starb 1235 im Alter von 92 Jahren in Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland und wurde in Balmerino, Fife, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

Lei avuto almeno 7 figlie con William 'The Lion' King of Scotland. Morì nel 1235, nel Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, all'età di 92 anni, e fu sepolta a Balmerino, Fife, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel d'Avenel(이)가 1143년 스컷랜드 에딘버그에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Avenel(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sybil(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 William 'The Lion' King of Scotland(와)과 적어도 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1235년 스컷랜드 스티를링실르 스티를링에서 92세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 피프 바머이노에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel d'Avenel nasceu em 1143, em Edinburgh, Scotland, seu pai, Robert Avenel, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Sybil, tinha 23 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 7 filhas com William 'The Lion' King of Scotland. Ela faleceu em 1235, em Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, com 92 anos, e foi sepultada em Balmerino, Fife, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.Langholm, Dumfries and Galloway, Scotlan: лангхолм, думфриесь анд галлоуеи, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel d'Avenel родилась в 1143 г. в местности эдинбург, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Robert Avenel было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Sybil, 23. У неё было по крайней мере 7 дочерей от William 'The Lion' King of Scotland. Она умерла в 1235 г. в местности стирлинг, стирлингшир, Шотландия в возрасте 92 лет и была похоронена в местности балмирино, фиф, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel d'Avenel nació en 1143, en Edinburgh, Scotland, su papá, Robert Avenel, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Sybil, tenía 23 años. Tuvo por lo menos 7 hijas con William 'The Lion' King of Scotland. Murió en 1235, en Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, a la edad de 92 años, y fue sepultada en Balmerino, Fife, Escocia, Reino Unido.
